faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth infestation in my Travis home?
faqs.answer: Signs of clothes moth infestation include small irregular holes in wool, silk, or fur garments, silken tubes or cases on fabric surfaces, adult moths flying near closets or storage areas (especially at dusk), and webbing in corners of closets or drawers. You may also notice larvae (small cream-colored caterpillars) or shed casings on affected materials. Our Travis technicians provide thorough inspections to assess the extent of any infestation.

faqs.question: What is included in your clothes moth control service for Travis properties?
faqs.answer: Our comprehensive service includes a detailed inspection of all potential infestation areas, identification of moth species and damage extent, targeted treatment of affected areas using EPA-approved products safe for use around fabrics, treatment of larvae and eggs in carpets and upholstery, recommendations for cleaning and prevention, and follow-up visits to ensure complete elimination. We customize our approach based on your specific situation and the layout of your Travis home.

faqs.question: Why does clothes moth control cost between $550 and $5,000?
faqs.answer: Pricing varies based on the severity of the infestation, the size of your Travis property, the number of rooms and storage areas affected, and whether treatment is needed for furniture, carpets, or just closets. Minor infestations in a single room may cost $550-$800, while whole-home treatments for severe infestations with extensive damage can reach $5,000. We provide free estimates and will clearly explain all costs before beginning treatment.

faqs.question: How can I prevent clothes moths from returning to my Travis home after treatment?
faqs.answer: Prevention includes regularly cleaning and vacuuming closets and storage areas, storing woolens and other susceptible fabrics in airtight containers or garment bags, periodically airing out stored clothing, maintaining low humidity levels (moths prefer 75% or higher), using cedar blocks or lavender sachets as natural deterrents, and inspecting secondhand clothing or furniture before bringing them into your home. Our technicians provide specific prevention guidance based on your Travis property's unique characteristics and will schedule follow-up inspections to ensure moths don't return.
